result:
Both issues are fixed and the project type-checks cleanly.

**Rate limiting.** The reset endpoints (`/auth/password-reset/request` and `/confirm`) already had a limiter (5/IP per 15 min), so the review was stale on that half â€” I verified and left it in place. The real gap was invitation acceptance: `POST /invitations/accept` and `/reject` take a secret token in the body with no limit, so tokens were brute-forceable. Both now share a new `invitationResponseLimiter` (10 attempts/IP per 15 min) in `src/workspaces/routes.ts`.

**Audit log.** New `audit_log` table in `src/db.ts` (append-only: event, actor, target, workspace, IP, user agent, JSONB details, with indexes on time/actor/event; user and workspace FKs are `ON DELETE SET NULL` so history survives deletions) and a `recordAudit()` helper in `src/audit.ts`. It accepts an optional transaction client so entries commit and roll back with the action they describe, and failures propagate rather than being swallowed â€” an action that can't be audited fails. Events recorded:

- `auth.signup`, `auth.login`, `auth.login_failed` (with attempted email and target user when it exists), `auth.logout`
- `auth.password_reset_requested` (logged for unknown emails too, so probing is visible, without changing the anti-enumeration response) and `auth.password_reset_completed`
- `role.granted` / `role.revoked` â€” covering the explicit assign/remove endpoints (written inside their transactions; the idempotent no-op re-grant is not logged) plus the two implicit grants: ADMIN on workspace creation and GUEST on invitation acceptance.

No secrets (passwords, tokens) ever enter the log. One thing worth knowing: signup itself is still unlimited; it wasn't in the review scope so I left it, but it's the same email-spam vector class if you want a follow-up.
